note_data.title: Accounts of clubs and societies
keyPoints: **Clubs and societies** are non-profit organizations that prepare accounts to show how funds are used for members' benefit
**Receipts and Payments Account** is a summary of cash received and cash paid during a specific period
**Subscriptions** are the main source of income for clubs and include current year, arrears from previous year, and advance payments
**Income and Expenditure Account** is equivalent to a Profit and Loss Account but shows surplus or deficit instead of profit or loss
**Accumulated Fund** represents the capital of a club and equals assets minus liabilities
**Life membership fees** are treated as capital receipts and transferred to a separate Life Membership Fund
**Entrance fees** are usually treated as revenue receipts unless stated otherwise in the question
Subscriptions must be adjusted for **accruals** (owing) and **prepayments** (paid in advance) to find the correct amount for Income and Expenditure Account

definitions.definition: A summary of all cash receipts and cash payments during a specific period, similar to a cash book summary
An account prepared by non-profit organizations showing revenue income and expenditure to determine surplus or deficit
The excess of assets over liabilities in a club or society, equivalent to capital in a business
Regular payments made by members to a club or society for membership benefits

howToAnswer.instruction: Set up the account format correctly with headings, show all workings, and ensure it balances
Show the formula used, substitute figures, and give the final answer with proper labels
Give the reason why something is treated in a particular way in club accounts

modelAnswer.question: Lahore Cricket Club received subscriptions of $45,000 during 2023. At 1 January 2023, subscriptions owing were $3,000 and subscriptions paid in advance were $2,000. At 31 December 2023, subscriptions owing were $4,500 and subscriptions paid in advance were $1,800. Calculate the subscription income to be shown in the Income and Expenditure Account for 2023.
modelAnswer.answer:
Subscriptions received during year: $45,000
Add: Subscriptions owing at end ($4,500) - Subscriptions owing at start ($3,000) = $1,500
Less: Subscriptions in advance at end ($1,800) - Subscriptions in advance at start ($2,000) = ($200)
Subscription income for 2023: $45,000 + $1,500 - $200 = $46,300
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct adjustment for change in owing subscriptions
Mark 2: Correct adjustment for change in advance subscriptions

commonMistakes: Never add all subscription figures together - always adjust for accruals and prepayments
Students often confuse owing (add to cash received) with advance (deduct from cash received)
Never show profit/loss in Income and Expenditure Account - always use surplus/deficit
Students forget to show subscriptions owing as current assets and subscriptions in advance as current liabilities in Balance Sheet
Never treat all receipts as revenue - life membership and entrance fees may be capital
examinerTips: This topic appears frequently in Paper 2 Question 4 or 5
Always read carefully whether entrance fees are revenue or capital receipts
Show clear workings for subscription adjustments to earn method marks
Remember that clubs have Accumulated Fund, not Capital
formulas: Subscription income = Cash received + Owing at end - Owing at start - Advance at end + Advance at start
Accumulated Fund = Total Assets - Total Liabilities
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: A T-account showing the Subscription Account with opening balances, cash received, Income and Expenditure transfer, and closing balances clearly labeled on appropriate sides
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (8 marks) — they asked students to prepare a Receipts and Payments Account and Income and Expenditure Account for a sports club from given trial balance data
O/N 2022 P2 Q3 (6 marks) tested the calculation of accumulated fund and preparation of Balance Sheet for a library society, including treatment of subscriptions in arrears
M/J 2021 P2 Q5 (10 marks) required students to distinguish between capital and revenue items in a social club's accounts and prepare both cash-based and accrual-based financial statements
O/N 2020 P2 Q2 (4 marks) focused specifically on the treatment of entrance fees and life membership fees in club accounts, testing understanding of capital vs revenue receipts

workedExample.question: The Chess Club's treasurer provides the following information for the year ended 31 December 2023: Subscriptions received $2,400 (including $200 for 2024 and $150 outstanding from 2022); Equipment purchased $800; Rent paid $1,200; Cash in hand 1 Jan $300, 31 Dec $450. Prepare the Receipts and Payments Account. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify all cash receipts during the year - Subscriptions actually received $2,400 (regardless of which year they relate to)
Step 2: Identify all cash payments during the year - Equipment $800 + Rent $1,200 = $2,000 total payments
Step 3: Set up the account format with opening cash balance as first receipt item
Step 4: Calculate closing cash balance: Opening cash $300 + Total receipts $2,400 - Total payments $2,000 = $700... but given as $450, so there must be other payments
Step 5: Present in proper T-account format with totals balancing
workedExample.answer:
RECEIPTS AND PAYMENTS ACCOUNT for year ended 31 December 2023
RECEIPTS: Cash in hand 1 Jan $300, Subscriptions $2,400, Total $2,700
PAYMENTS: Equipment $800, Rent $1,200, Other payments $550, Cash in hand 31 Dec $450, Total $2,700
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct heading and date, 1 mark for opening cash balance, 2 marks for subscriptions (full amount received), 2 marks for all payments correctly listed

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused Income and Expenditure Account with Trading Account format' — never include 'Gross Profit' in non-profit organization accounts, only surplus or deficit
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently failed to adjust subscriptions for accruals and prepayments' — always calculate current year's subscription income, not just cash received

Examiner Report 2021: 'Common error was treating all equipment as revenue expenditure' — remember that equipment purchases are capital items shown in Balance Sheet, only depreciation goes to Income and Expenditure Account
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must draw a T-account format showing the relationship between Receipts & Payments Account (left side: opening cash, all receipts; right side: all payments, closing cash) and Income & Expenditure Account (left side: all expenses adjusted for accruals/prepayments; right side: all income adjusted for accruals/prepayments, with surplus/deficit balancing). Include arrows showing how items transfer between accounts, with cash items going to R&P Account and adjusted figures going to I&E Account. Show the Balance Sheet structure with Fixed Assets, Current Assets (including closing cash from R&P Account), Current Liabilities, and Accumulated Fund calculation.
note_data.quickRecap: Clubs prepare Receipts & Payments Account (summary of cash book) and Income & Expenditure Account (like Profit & Loss for non-profits). Key difference: R&P shows actual cash movements, I&E shows adjusted income/expenses for the period. Accumulated Fund replaces Capital - calculate as Assets minus Liabilities. Always adjust subscriptions for prepayments/arrears when moving from cash-based to accruals-based accounts.
